description: Producer: Stripe success path in recordExternalPayment enqueues record_payment when a realm is connected and the invoice is mapped; unmapped invoice → skipped with stat, not exception.

record_payment op execution: QBO Payment with CustomerRef from client mapping, Line linking the mapped invoice, PaymentRefNum = Stripe reference, DepositToAccountRef from tenant setting (default Undeposited Funds resolved at delivery).
Payment mapping row written at push time so the next CDC poll treats the pushed payment as already-applied (echo suppression).
getQboAccounts server action (deposit-eligible accounts, catalog pattern) + deposit-account picker in QBO settings.
record_payment failures (invoice deleted in QBO, invalid account) file accounting_sync_export_error exceptions via the slice-1 framework.
Double-entry guard verified: a QBO-side manual duplicate of a pushed payment surfaces as an exception on the already-settled invoice rather than double-applying.
getQboClasses and getQboDepartments server actions (catalog pattern, realm-scoped, cached).
Tenant default class + department settings with pickers in QBO settings.
Item mapping metadata supports classId; mapping dialog documents it; items tab renders a class column when present.
Invoice transform emits per-line SalesItemLineDetail.ClassRef (item metadata → tenant default → omit) and header DepartmentRef (tenant default → omit); CreditMemo transform mirrors it.
default_realm tenant setting consumed by getDefaultQboRealmId (explicit default replaces first-stored-key ordering); make-default action in settings.
Settings connection list renders one row per connected realm (company name, status, last cycle) replacing the single-connection card when realm count > 1.
Realm picker in the batch creation dialog and the slice-3 wizard, shown only when more than one realm is connected.
Cycle scheduling enumerates realms: connect registers that realm's cycle, disconnect deregisters only that realm; per-realm cursors/health already from slice 1.
Realm-scoped surfaces (badge aggregation, health panel, mapping tabs) consistently read the selected/default realm; single-realm tenants see no UI change.
i18n: deposit/class/department pickers, connection list, realm pickers in en + translated locales; pseudo-locales regenerated.
